TURN: Practice Makes People – Living Lent
episodes.description: With Canon Broderick Greer  While "What are you giving up for Lent?" is good small talk at coffee hour, the focus on abstaining from chocolate or Starbucks misses God's broader invitation to a holy Lent: The taking on, not just giving up of practices that awaken us to God's liberating love in Christ. Join Canon Broderick as he explores a set of practices that might enhance your experience of this sacred season.

episodes.title: The Last Sunday after the Epiphany
February 23 TURN: Rhyme and Reason – Preparing for Ash Wednesday
episodes.description: With Canon Broderick Greer  The baptized life is sustained by a rhythm of work and rest, of fasting and feasting. As we stand on the brink of Lent, Canon Broderick explores with us how this rhythm informs our collective and individual preparations for Ash Wednesday.

GO: The Search for Peace and Justice in the Holy Land

LEARN: Why Formation?
episodes.description: With Tina Clark, Director of Formation  This journey of faith is always a place of mystery and wonder, of faith balanced sometimes precariously with doubt; we are always on a wilderness road. Tina explores how Christian Formation takes us not off the path of wilderness, but instead invites each of us to walk in community and wonder, finding God with and within at every turn.

episodes.title: LEARN: Why Formation?
episodes.description: With Tina Clark, Director of Formation This journey of faith is always a place of mystery and wonder, of faith balanced sometimes precariously with doubt; we are always on a wilderness road. Tina explores how Christian Formation takes us not off the path of wilderness, but instead invites each of us to walk in community and wonder, finding God with and within at every turn.

episodes.title: THE WAY OF LOVE: Practices for a Jesus Centered Life
episodes.description: With the Saint John's Cathedral Convention Delegates  What happens at the annual convention of The Episcopal Church in Colorado? What are Bishop Kym Lucas’ goals and agenda as she settles in here? What is this “Way of Love” people are talking about? Amanda Barker and a panel of our Saint John’s convention delegates discuss convention and the path of our diocese as we begin a new year together.

episodes.title: Dean's Forum – THE WAY OF LOVE: Practices for a Jesus Centered Life
episodes.description: What happens at the annual convention of The Episcopal Church in Colorado? What are Bishop Kym Lucas' goals and agenda as she settles into her role as Bishop? What is this "Way of Love" people are talking about? Amanda Barker and a panel of our Saint John's convention delegates discuss convention and the path of our diocese as we begin a new year together.

episodes.title: Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory – Purgatory
episodes.description: Dean Richard discusses why purgatory is not an official Episcopal or Anglican doctrine. However, many of our beloved prayers in the current Prayer Book suggest that in the afterlife the soul travels a long journey that takes an eternity to complete; this journey leads us more deeply into the mystery of divine love in Christ.

episodes.title: Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory – Hell
episodes.description: Dean Richard leads a class on what he considers to be one of the most dangerous concepts in the history of Christianity: hell. Richard examines how hell imagined as a literal place has caused confusion and psychological torment throughout the ages. He discusses why the Episcopal Church, as well as the church’s ancient creeds, focus upon divine mercy rather than punishment in the afterlife.

episodes.title: Heaven, Hell, and Purgatory – Heaven
episodes.description: Tina Clark leads a class on the mystery of heaven, which is something that Jesus describes as being “already among you” (Luke 17:21) and yet as something beyond death (John 12:24-25). She explores heaven as something we should imagine and yet as something that is unfathomable.
episodes.author_field: Tina Clark, Director of Christian Formation

episodes.title: Last Sunday after Pentecost: Christ the King
Dean's Forum: Into the Weeds Again: Creeds and Baptism
episodes.description: In the Daily Office, Sunday Eucharists, burials, and baptisms, Christians proclaim the Trinitarian faith of the Church as articulated in the Apostles' and Nicene Creeds, a regular reminder of the claim the Holy Trinity makes on our lives in the waters of baptism. Join us as we mine the interplay between the creeds and the sacrament of baptism.

episodes.title: Dean's Forum: Context and Imagination: Reading the Bible Today
episodes.description: With the Rev. Broderick Greer, Canon Precentor The roles of context and imagination are central to Episcopal biblical interpretation because Episcopalians take the Bible too seriously to take it literally.  The Bible, like any collection of texts in the wrong hands, can be used to prop up systems of violence. Join us as we explore humane, gentle, and transformative interpretations of a book that remains at the top of many bestseller lists.

episodes.title: Dean's Forum: Cultivating Competence and Compassion in Dementia Care
episodes.description: As dementia continues to rise in epidemic numbers in our country, so too, does the challenge of providing competent care to those most impacted in our families and church communities.  It is therefore vital to understand the implications that this disease has on grief, loss, and our most significant relationships, so that we can compassionately support people living with dementia and their care partners through this long and challenging illness. Kay M. Adams is a Licensed Clinical Social...

episodes.title: Dean's Forum: Biblical Fracking: Midrash for the Modern Christian
The Sixteenth Sunday after Pentecost - The Wilderness
Dean's Forum: In Our Own Voice
episodes.description: With Brenda Magsamen, NAMI (National Alliance on Mental Illness) Development and Special Project Coordinator for Colorado In Our Own Voice: Living with Mental Illness is NAMI's national effort to educate the general public and change the attitudes, preconceived notions, and stereotypes of how persons living with mental illness look and behave.

episodes.title: Dean's Forum: Pilgrimage to Peace Tour
episodes.description: Join Churches for Middle East Peace (CMEP) Executive Director Rev. Dr. Mae Elise Cannon as she hosts a conversation with Rabbi Dr. Daniel Roth from Israel and Palestinian-American Aziz Abu Sarah, about multifaith dialogue and engagement in constructive conflict, peace, and the reality of life in Israel and Palestine. We discuss the role we can play in hosting dialogues about disagreements and becoming peacemakers and reconcilers in the midst of conflict. We believe we can bring hope to broken...

The Seating of Bishop Kym Lucas
Abraham's Children: What We Share and How We Differ - Dr. Hamza Yusef
episodes.description: The family of Abraham traditionally included the three faiths, but lately, many people see Islam as an illegitimate member of the family, unwelcome in the house of Abraham. Historically, for Christians, this role was relegated to the Jewish members as "disinherited" siblings. Jews, however, for most Christians at least, have become full members of the family in good standing. Is this a possibility for the Muslims, or does the Quranic verse "The Jews and the Christians will never be pleased wi...

episodes.title: Is Islam an American Religion? -  Dr. Hamza Yusuf
episodes.description: The Everding Lectures Presented by Dr. Hamza Yusuf, President and Senior Faculty Member, Zaytuna College   Since 9/11 over two million Muslims have died in conflicts that in one way or another were related to the events in September 2001. Muslims have been the greatest victims of terror, with minority communities in the Middle East a distant second. In 1947, the historian, Prof. Arnold Toynbee, foretold of a militant version of Islam coupled with a nihilistic zealotry arising in the not ...

episodes.title: The Second Sunday in Lent - The Wilderness
Honest to God: Sensible Spirituality w/ the Rev. Dr. Sam Portaro
episodes.description: We are thrilled to welcome to Saint John's the Reverend Dr. Sam A. Portaro, an Episcopal priest, author, and faculty member at the CREDO institute.  Each of us in the church claims relationship with God; it's the basis of Baptism and reaffirmed every time we recite any Christian creed. Every relationship entails communication of some kind, often of many different kinds. As former Presiding Bishop Frank Griswold has noted, "Theology is how we talk about God; spirituality is how we talk with Go...

The Twenty Second Sunday after Pentecost
episodes.description: The Very Reverend Dr. Alan W. Jones is Dean Emeritus at Grace Cathedral in San Francisco. Previously, he served as the Stephen F. Bayne Professor of Ascetical Theology at the General Theological Seminary in New York City, where he was the founder of the Center of Christian Spirituality at General. Dr. Jones is the author of numerous books, including "Journey Into Christ" and "Exploring Spiritual Direction: An Essay in Christian Friendship."
episodes.author_field: The Very Reverend Dr. Alan W. Jones

episodes.title: Desmond Tutu and The Quest for Racial Forgiveness in South Africa and the USA
episodes.description:
The second of two Everding Lectures presented by Dr. Tinyiko Maluleke, Professor of Theology at the University of Pretoria, South Africa. 
There is overwhelming evidence that when Desmond Tutu accepted the role of chairperson of the South African Truth and Reconciliation Commission, it was for him much more than the acceptance of a presidential appointment. He saw it as an opportunity to ritualize, exemplify and advocate the power of forgiveness and reconciliation in the building of nation an...

episodes.title: "I Am Prepared to Die" Nelson Mandela and the Practice of Hope and Integrity
episodes.description:
The first of two Everding Lectures presented by Dr. Tinyiko Maluleke, Professor of Theology at the University of Pretoria, South Africa.  

What kept Mandela hopeful and energetic all through the dark days of the struggle against Apartheid? Where did Mandela get the courage to make the daring speech in April 1964 before a 'white man's court' which had the power to sentence him to death?
